title: Maintaining The Body | HG Amitasana Prabhu
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So how not to get fooled by Maya is what we're talking about. This is no way to say that I am
not the body and hence I completely do not take care of the body. Sometimes people go to that
extreme. After they come to this knowledge, they stop exercising, they stop going for a walk,
stop going for taking care of the body because I am not the body, which is a wrong understanding.
So in the Gita, Krishna talks about Kshetra and Kshetragna. Kshetra means a body,
Yantra and Kshetragna means the real occupier of the body. Kshetra is very important.
Just like I gave example of a car. To identify with the car is wrong, but car can be used very
nicely. And car being a Yantra follows certain laws. Like for example, even if you are using
car for Lord Seva, from time to time, you have to check the tire pressures, air pressure,
you have to send it to the service station. One day you come for a program, Abhishek is going on,
you collect your Namrit and you put in that.
Petrol tank and says, Charnaamit, you also get a little spiritual. Will it work?
It follows certain laws. Now, if you don't take care of your car, even if you want to use it for
Lord service, a time will come if you're not servicing it, if you're not checking it properly,
this thing, it will stop functioning properly. So two extremes we have to avoid when you say
that I am not the body. One extreme is, I am not the body, so I am reckless about,
I want to treat today as anybody. You have to quiet down your Kiaeka with the same kind of
belief, that youresser thinks I am a lucky one. So, you have to calm down. Seven steps
to take more dynamically, you must understand that the physical touch.
Bit of additional side effect on the body, it is the Ehre, all the kinds of things inside,
the collection.
There are thousands of reasons to use the head,做
not the body, so I don't care of the body. I'm indifferent to the body. I neglect the body. I
ignore the bodily needs. That is wrong. The other extreme is I am the body and then I enroll for a
gym and every day two hours, three hours, six pack, that's my goal. That's also wrong. Body
is a yantra which has been given to us. I am residing in that yantra. I am not that yantra.
I have to maintain that yantra in good shape for me to do the real business.
For which, if 15, 20 minutes, 30 minutes of exercise, stretching, Surya Namaskar,
all that is required, I do it. Yes. So is that clear, first thing? So I am not the body does
not mean that, now I am not the body does not mean that Krishna is recommending that we stop eating,
we stop seeing. Anything I see, anything I eat which is pleasurable to taste, that is Maya. No.
Again,
let me avoid two extremes. The goal should not become that, my goal is to seek pleasure
through my tongue and I eat. I always seek for all pleasurable things, no matter from
which source they come, whether it's veg, non-veg, so long as it is tasting for my taste buds,
I take it. That's another extreme.
The other extreme is, no, no, I am not the body. Anything which is tasty for my tongue,
it has to be shunned. It has to be renounced. That's other extreme.
